stimulus: Umm Kulthum was one of the most influential Egyptian singers of the twentieth century. Her wide-ranging concert programs—which combined classical Arabic forms, such as tarab and the qasida, with newly composed orchestral arrangements—gave Umm Kulthum a lasting place in the Arab musical imagination, ______blank her the title "the Voice of Egypt."
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The word "earning" is being used to introduce an additional modifying phrase that describes how Umm Kulthum’s programs lent her a lasting place. When a verb ends in -ing and doesn’t have a helper verb like "is" before it, it can be used to modify a noun or verb, which is appropriate here.

Choice A is incorrect. The underlined verb is being used to introduce additional descriptive information set off by a comma. Because there is no new subject here, a finite form of the verb (like "had earned") creates an error. Choice C is incorrect. Because there is no new subject here, the finite verb "earn" creates an error; a non-finite form is needed. Choice D is incorrect. Because there is no new subject here, the finite past-tense verb "earned" creates an error; a non-finite form is needed.
